prompt.raw:
[{"content":"You are a teaching assistant analyzing a teacher's audio transcript about student observations.

Your task:
1. Identify which students are mentioned in the transcript
2. Match each mentioned name to the student roster below (handle phonetic/partial matches)
3. Extract the date if mentioned (format YYYY-MM-DD), otherwise leave empty
4. Write a summary per student capturing what the teacher said about them
   - Clean up speech artifacts (false starts, filler words, repetitions) into clear sentences
   - Preserve the teacher's voice, tone, and specific observations — do NOT add details or opinions not present in the transcript
   - Keep the teacher's vocabulary and perspective (first person if they used it)
   - Combine multiple mentions of the same student into a cohesive note

Student Roster:
- Emma Torres (class Class A)
- Ryan Mitchell (class Class A)
- Lila Patel (class Class A)

Rules:
- Match mentioned names against the roster even if pronunciation differs slightly
- Some roster entries include "(aka ...)" aliases — if a teacher uses an alias, match it to the canonical name and return the canonical name in the "name" field
- Set confidence 0.0-1.0 for each match. Use >= 0.7 for confident matches.
- If confidence < 0.7, include up to 3 closest roster matches in "candidates"
- A student is "individually mentioned" ONLY if the teacher uses their name (or a recognizable nickname/variant of their name). Generic group references like "everyone", "all students", "the class" do NOT count as individual mentions.
- Do NOT create entries for students who are never individually mentioned by name. If a student is only covered by group-level observations (e.g. "the class was loud") but never called out by name, they must NOT appear in the output.
- For students who ARE individually mentioned by name, their quoted_text MUST include BOTH their individual observations AND any group-level observations from the same transcript/class. This applies to EVERY individually-mentioned student in that class, regardless of where the group observation appears in the transcript (beginning, middle, or end) or which students were named immediately before/after it. Do not attach group observations only to the students mentioned closest to them — propagate them to all named students in the class.
  Example: Transcript says "Alice did great. Bob was quiet. The whole class struggled with fractions." → BOTH Alice's and Bob's quoted_text must include the fractions observation, not just Bob's.
- If the transcript contains group references like "everyone", "all students", or "the class", apply those observations only to students in the class being discussed, not to ALL classes. Use context clues (class name mentions, prior student mentions) to determine which class is meant.
- For multi-student transcripts, produce a separate entry per student with relevant passages
- If a mentioned student cannot be matched to any roster entry, do not include them in the output
- If no students are clearly mentioned, return an empty students array
- The "class" field for each student MUST exactly match one of the class names from the roster above. Do not invent or abbreviate class names.
- IMPORTANT: Clean up speech into readable sentences, but do NOT invent observations or editorialize. Stay faithful to what the teacher actually said.
","role":"system"},{"content":"Um, so today I wanted to talk about Emma. She, uh, she did great in math today, really got the hang of the multiplication tables we've been working on. And then, let's see... I also wanted to mention Ryan. Ryan was, he was really struggling with the reading comprehension exercise, couldn't quite get the main idea from the passage. Yeah, that's, that's about it for today.","role":"user"}]
prompt.label: gradebee-prompt

students.name: Emma Torres
students.quoted_text: Emma did great in math today and really got the hang of the multiplication tables we've been working on.

students.name: Ryan Mitchell
students.quoted_text: Ryan was really struggling with the reading comprehension exercise and couldn't quite get the main idea from the passage.

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Zayneb
Teacher: Marcia
Level: Marcia-Wed@14:10
Absences: -
Frequency of use (audio): -

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Excellent

Comment: Zayneb brings a consistently positive and confident energy to the classroom. She is always eager to participate and shows enthusiasm in all activities, including story retelling, charades, and workbook tasks. She regularly volunteers answers and remains engaged throughout lessons, contributing to a focused and productive learning environment. Zayneb pushes herself to always give her very best, and this determination is clearly reflected in her active involvement and strong classroom presence.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Excellent

Comment: Zayneb has made outstanding progress in her learning and shows a very secure understanding of the topics covered. She consistently answers questions correctly and uses complete sentences with confidence. She is able to help repeat and retell the story, and she remembers what Marcia needs to wear to go to different places. Her work during activities such as “Can you please...?” examples, running, jumping, and hiking was excellent, showing both strong comprehension and very good language use. Zayneb repeats everything in full structures and demonstrates a clear ability to apply what she has learned.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Comment: Zayneb’s behaviour in class is excellent. She follows instructions well, works calmly through workbook activities, and remains focused during group games. She is cooperative and supportive, often helping the class by repeating parts of the story and contributing appropriately during discussions. Her positive attitude and respectful manner contribute to a smooth and productive classroom environment. Zayneb’s behaviour is consistently exemplary.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 9.50  Excellent. Zayneb can understand the question and answers confidently in complete structures.

Final comment

Zayneb has had an outstanding year and should be very proud of her progress. She demonstrates excellent participation, a secure understanding of the content, and exemplary behaviour in class. Her enthusiasm, confidence, and willingness to give her best in every activity make her a pleasure to teach. She has made excellent progress and is well prepared for the next stage of her learning.

Data

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Thomas usually comes to class in a quiet mood. He can sometimes be a bit hesitant to join in at the beginning of the lesson and needs to be encouraged to take part in the routines. However, when motivated, he generally shows interest in the activities and seems to enjoy the story time. This term, he particularly responded well to the story "Why are you so sad" and was able to repeat the phrases very well. He can also be attentive and work well when he is focused on the activity.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Thomas's English knowledge is good. He can show that he understands the vocabulary and structures learnt in class, especially the moods, the genders and the weather. He can also repeat the weather and do the gestures correctly. Thomas is able to count very well and can pair the dot cards with the corresponding numbers. He sometimes needs a bit of help to remember the full structure or to identify the animal on his card, but he is able to tell the colour and often understands what is expected of him. He also did the test very well.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:
Thomas is usually a calm student. At times, he can be a little uncooperative and needs reminders to stay focused and follow the teacher's instructions. Nevertheless, he generally responds well when guided and is able to work properly with the group. He enjoys the activities and usually takes part well once he is settled. He should continue to learn to handle the material with more care and to participate more actively during class.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.50    Good.

---

Throughout the school year, Thomas has shown that he can understand and reproduce the language learnt in class when he is focused and encouraged to participate. He has made progress in his vocabulary knowledge, his comprehension and his confidence with the different routines. It is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays in order to keep in mind the vocabulary words and structures learnt throughout the school year.

---

Throughout the school year, Thomas has shown that he can understand and reproduce the language learnt in class when he is focused and encouraged to participate. Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

Name: Emma
Teacher: Huryv Osmanova
Level: Fairy Tales 2 Tuesday & Friday 2025/2026
Absences: 0

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Excellent

Emma is a polite and well-mannered student who generally shows a positive attitude in class. She is calm, respectful, and attentive, and she works well with both her teacher and classmates. At times, she can be a little hesitant to speak up, but with encouragement she is willing to try and becomes more confident. She responds well when given time to think and can be very receptive to guidance. Her kind and cooperative nature contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Work

Assessment (Work): Good

Emma takes part in class activities and usually completes the work she is asked to do with care. She has shown good effort throughout the term, especially when speaking and working with familiar structures. She can make sentences using the target language and is able to follow the lesson content well. However, she sometimes needs encouragement to try more independently and to avoid relying too much on French. Continuing to build confidence and expanding her vocabulary will help her participate more freely and consistently.

Performance

Assessment (Performance): Good

Emma has made good progress this term. She understands the new vocabulary well and can recognise and use many of the structures covered in class. She is also able to read the stories and chapters well, and her reading is often impressive. In oral activities, she can answer using full structures and usually corrects herself when she makes mistakes, which is very positive. She still needs to work on pronunciation, connectors, and some grammar points such as the third person singular, pronouns, and tense accuracy. With regular practice and more confidence, she will continue to improve.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.10 Good.

Emma performs well orally when she feels comfortable and has enough time to think. She can repeat and use the target language accurately in many situations, and her pronunciation is generally improving. However, she still makes occasional pronunciation mistakes and sometimes needs support to expand her answers.

Writing Mark: 6.80 Good.

Emma's written work shows a good understanding of the language covered this term. She can complete grammar and vocabulary tasks quite well. However, she still makes some errors with capital letters, punctuation, and grammar when writing independently. Further practice with writing and careful revision before tests will help her gain more confidence and accuracy.

Homework mark: 6.50 Acceptable.

Emma has completed some homework and has generally shown a reasonable effort. To improve further, she should make a more consistent effort to practise at home, revise vocabulary regularly, and review the grammar points covered in class.

Final comment

Over the course of the term, Emma has shown a positive attitude and has made steady progress in class. She is polite, attentive, and increasingly willing to try, especially when she is given a bit of time and support. Her reading skills are particularly strong, and she shows good understanding of the content covered in class. To continue improving, she should focus on speaking more confidently, using English as much as possible, and revising vocabulary and grammar regularly. With continued encouragement and practice, Emma is well placed to make further progress next term.

Data

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Chloé usually comes to class in a good mood and is generally willing to take part in the activities. Chloé enjoys story time and can often repeat the story with the teacher and the group. She also participates well in the song and worksheet activities, especially when she feels reassured. At times, she can be a little insistent and keep asking for confirmation even when she already knows what to do, but she responds well when redirected and then works more calmly with the rest of the class.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory

Comment:

Chloé's English knowledge is satisfactory. She can repeat the structures worked on in class and understands several of the vocabulary items introduced this term. She has learnt to identify and use the different clothes items, colours and body parts worked on in the course, as well as the concepts of clean/dirty and wet/dry. She can also tell her age and gender using a complete structure with relative confidence. Chloé is able to complete the worksheet activities and to match or place the different items correctly, sometimes needing a little help to recall the vocabulary or the exact structure expected. She benefits from regular revision to consolidate the clothing vocabulary and to become more independent when answering.

Behaviour

Comment:

Chloé is an attentive, cheerful and lively student. She is generally well integrated within the group and can work cooperatively with her classmates and her teacher. Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Chloé
Teacher: Christine Blois
Level: Emma Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:

Chloé usually comes to class in a good mood and is generally willing to take part in the activities. She shows interest in the different workshop tasks and, once she is reminded of the rules or the expected place, she is able to join in very well. Chloé enjoys story time and can often repeat the story with the teacher and the group. She also participates well in the song and worksheet activities, especially when she feels reassured. At times, she can be a little insistent and keep asking for confirmation even when she already knows what to do, but she responds well when redirected and then works more calmly with the rest of the class.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Satisfactory

Comment:

Chloé's English knowledge is satisfactory. She can repeat the structures worked on in class and understands several of the vocabulary items introduced this term. She has learnt to identify and use the different clothes items, colours and body parts worked on in the course, as well as the concepts of clean/dirty and wet/dry. She can also tell her age and gender using a complete structure with relative confidence. Chloé is able to complete the worksheet activities and to match or place the different items correctly, sometimes needing a little help to recall the vocabulary or the exact structure expected. She benefits from regular revision to consolidate the clothing vocabulary and to become more independent when answering.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:

Chloé is an attentive, cheerful and lively student. She is generally well integrated within the group and can work cooperatively with her classmates and her teacher. At the beginning of the lesson, or when the class is a little noisy, she may need to be reminded to focus and listen carefully to instructions, but she usually settles down quickly when the teacher puts clear boundaries in place. She can sometimes become a little distracted or tired, yet this does not prevent her from participating appropriately once she has been guided back to the task. She treats the classroom material correctly and is able to follow the lesson rhythm well.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 6.80 Good. Chloé can understand the question and usually answers with a suitable structure, especially for the language worked on this term. She sometimes needs a small reminder to use the full sentence, but she shows a good understanding of the story and the new vocabulary. Keep up the good work, Chloé!

---

Final comment

Throughout the term, Chloé has shown a pleasant attitude in class and has gradually become more confident when taking part in the activities. She has made good progress with the story, the clothes vocabulary and the structures linked to the unit. With continued listening and regular revision, she will be able to strengthen her vocabulary recall and answer more independently. It is recommended to listen to the audio tracks during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary and structures learnt throughout the school year.

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name: Mila
Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
Level: Oliver Thursday 2025/2026
Absences: 0
Frequency of use (audio): Insufficient

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Mila generally comes to class in a positive mood and often shows enthusiasm for the activities. She is usually willing to participate, especially in more dynamic tasks such as action activities, games, and routines she knows well. She enjoys being involved and, at times, likes to help with classroom materials, which reflects her interest in the lesson. However, when things do not go her way or when she feels frustrated, she can withdraw and become less cooperative. With continued encouragement, Mila can keep building a more consistent and open attitude towards all classroom activities.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Mila has shown good understanding of the vocabulary and structures worked on this term. She is able to repeat the language confidently and can already use several of the new words and sentence patterns in context. She remembers much of the story and can retell parts of it with very little help, which shows good progress in her learning. She also performs very well in activities where she can match language with actions or visual support. Mila is still developing her confidence with some weather-related vocabulary, particularly the expressions “taking off” and “putting on”, and she sometimes needs support when new material is introduced or when she is asked to stay focused until the end of an activity. We will continue to reinforce these areas so that she can keep progressing with greater independence.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Satisfactory

Comment:
Mila is well integrated in the group and usually enjoys working with her classmates. At times, though, she can become upset when she is asked to wait, when she cannot have something she wants, or when she does not feel comfortable during the activity. On a few occasions, this affected her behaviour, and she needed reminders to sit properly and remain engaged with the lesson. Once she calms down, she is generally able to rejoin the class and take part again. Mila responds well to structured and active tasks, and her behaviour improves when she feels supported and encouraged.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 7.50 Good. Mila can understand the question and answers with a structure which is generally correct, although she may still need some support with less familiar vocabulary.

Final comment

________________________________________________________________________________

Mila has made good progress this term and has shown that she understands and can use much of the language worked on in class. She is a lively student who enjoys participating and can do very well when she feels confident and engaged. With continued practice and support in managing moments of frustration, she will keep moving forward with confidence. Well done, Mila.

Objectives and content of the Oliver course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

  • Name: Camille
  • Teacher: Thérèse Garnier
  • Level: Oliver Group 2 Saturday 2025/2026

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation): Good

Comment:
Camille generally comes into class in a positive and willing mood, showing interest in the activities and a good attitude towards the lesson. She enjoys joining in, especially when the tasks are playful and familiar, such as Present for me, Who’s missing, Family Bingo and the Dice Game. Camille is cooperative with the teacher and with the group, and she shows a clear desire to take part in what is happening in class. At times she can be a little playful with the material, but this remains manageable. Overall, her motivation is good and she is usually open to new activities.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process): Good

Comment:
Camille shows a good level of understanding of the story and the language worked on in class. She could recall parts of the story and she showed that she had memorised a number of key vocabulary items, including colours, clothing and some prepositions. She also understood the worksheet activity well and completed it successfully, particularly the work on what Oliver and Baby could or couldn’t do. Camille is beginning to use the structures worked on during the story, although she still needs support to remember the correct forms in some activities. She benefits from reminders and repetition, but she shows real comprehension and a solid memory for the vocabulary. Her learning is progressing well, and she continues to build confidence with the new story and structures.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour): Good

Comment:
Camille is well integrated within the group and usually behaves appropriately in class. She is attentive and listens well, particularly when the lesson is engaging, and she responds positively to encouragement. On one occasion she was a little distracting with Zoé, and at times she did not always give the material back immediately when asked, but this was not a major issue. She can become a little more hesitant on some days, but with support she remains calm and receptive. Overall, her behaviour is good and she contributes positively to the classroom atmosphere.

Marks

Oral Test Mark: 8.20    Good. Camille can understand the question and answers with a structure which is mostly correct, showing a good command of the vocabulary and story content.

Final comment
Camille has had a very positive term. She has shown good motivation, strong attention during many activities and a solid understanding of the story and vocabulary. She enjoys participating and is often enthusiastic, especially in the games and routines. Although she still needs occasional help to remember the correct structures, she is progressing well and showing increasing confidence. Well done, Camille, and keep up the good work.
